description: Sample glossary for LSP testing, combining common tags, conceptual types, and a representative set of metrics from the ontology.

nf.account.description: AWS account ID for the metric.

nf.region.description: AWS region where the instance is running.

nf.zone.description: Datacenter in an AWS region. Netflix typically uses three zones.

nf.ami.description: Base image used to launch an instance.

nf.app.description: Application name. Used to associate metadata like owner info.

nf.cluster.description: Specific deployment of an application, typically app-stack-detail.

nf.shard1.description: Operational partition dimension. Limited to alphanumeric characters.

nf.asg.description: Server group or auto-scaling group of instances representing a service.

nf.node.description: Instance identifier (EC2 instance ID or Titus task ID).

nf.process.description: Process identifier. Requires NETFLIX_PROCESS_NAME environment variable.

nf.country.description: ISO country code. Use cautiously as it can increase metrics volume.

nf.country.rollup.description: Business-level country grouping (e.g. US, EMEA, APAC, LATAM).

statistic.description: Identifies the component of a conceptual type (count, totalTime, gauge, etc.).

id.description: Sub-identifier within a metric. Meaning is metric-specific.

statistic=count.description: Rate of events per second. Present on counters, timers, distribution summaries, and interval counters.

statistic=totalTime.description: Total time in seconds spent on events per second. Present on timers.

statistic=totalOfSquares.description: Sum of squared measurements per second. Used with count and totalTime to compute standard deviation. Present on timers and distribution summaries.

statistic=max.description: Maximum value recorded in the polling interval. Present on timers and distribution summaries.

statistic=gauge.description: Sampled value at a point in time. Present on gauges.

statistic=activeTasks.description: Number of currently in-progress long tasks. Present on long task timers.

statistic=duration.description: Total duration of all active tasks in seconds. Present on long task timers and interval counters.

statistic=totalAmount.description: Total amount recorded per second (units vary). Present on distribution summaries.

statistic=percentile.description: Used for modeling distribution to approximate percentiles. Present on timers and distribution summaries.

apache.http.request.description: Timer generated from parsing request logs indicating the latency for serving requests from Apache.

aws.alb.activeConnectionCount.description: The total number of concurrent TCP connections active from clients to the load balancer and from the load balancer to targets.

aws.alb.backendResponses.description: Number of HTTP response codes generated by targets, excluding load balancer responses.

aws.alb.healthyHostCount.description: The number of targets that are considered healthy.

aws.alb.requestCount.description: Number of requests processed over IPv4 and IPv6.

aws.alb.targetResponseTime.description: Time elapsed after the request leaves the load balancer until a response from the target is received.

aws.ec2.cpuUtilization.description: Percentage of allocated EC2 compute units that are currently in use on the instance.

aws.ec2.networkIn.description: Number of bytes received on all network interfaces by the instance.

aws.ec2.networkOut.description: Number of bytes sent out on all network interfaces by the instance.

aws.ec2.diskReadBytes.description: Bytes read from all instance store volumes available to the instance.

aws.dynamodb.consumedCapacity.description: The number of read capacity units consumed.

aws.dynamodb.throttledRequests.description: Requests to DynamoDB that exceed the provisioned throughput limits.

aws.elasticache.cpuUtilization.description: The percentage of CPU utilization for the entire host.

aws.elasticache.currConnections.description: The number of client connections, excluding connections from read replicas.

aws.elasticache.cacheHits.description: The number of successful read-only key lookups in the main dictionary.

aws.elasticache.evictions.description: The number of non-expired items the cache evicted due to the memory limit.

aws.rds.databaseConnections.description: The number of database connections in use.

aws.rds.freeableMemory.description: The amount of available random access memory.

aws.sqs.numberOfMessagesReceived.description: Number of messages returned by calls to the ReceiveMessage action.

aws.sqs.approximateNumberOfMessagesVisib.description: The number of messages available for retrieval from the queue.

ipc.client.call.description: Timer recording the latency of outgoing IPC calls.

ipc.vip.description: Virtual IP (VIP) or service name of the target.

ipc.server.call.description: Timer recording the latency of incoming IPC calls handled by the server.

jvm.gc.pause.description: Timer recording the pause time for JVM garbage collection events.

jvm.gc.allocationRate.description: Rate at which memory is being allocated by the JVM.

jvm.gc.promotionRate.description: Rate at which data is being promoted from young to old generation.

jvm.gc.liveDataSize.description: Size of old generation memory pool after a full GC.

jvm.gc.maxDataSize.description: Maximum size of old generation memory pool.

jvm.memory.max.description: The maximum amount of memory that can be used.

jvm.buffer.memoryUsed.description: An estimate of the memory that the JVM is using for buffer pools.

jvm.thread.count.description: The current number of live threads including both daemon and non-daemon threads.

sys.net.bytes.description: Number of bytes transmitted or received on a network interface.

nodejs.eventloop.lag.description: Event loop lag for Node.js applications.

spectator.measurements.description: Counter indicating the number of measurements reported by the Spectator library.

spectator.registrySize.description: Number of meters currently registered in the Spectator registry.
